column1: Data Dictionary — Indian Passenger EV Market Analytics Workbook

Scope: officially launched, mainstream, passenger Battery Electric Vehicles (BEVs) sold in India (Hatchback, Sedan, SUV, MPV, Crossover). Excludes two/three-wheelers, buses, trucks, commercial EVs and luxury marques. Designed to load cleanly into PostgreSQL and power a Streamlit analytics dashboard.
Sheet: EV_Models — one row per unique EV model sold in India

column1: • Scope filter: only officially launched, mainstream, India-market passenger BEVs. Two/three-wheelers, buses, trucks, commercial-only EVs, and luxury marques (BMW, Mercedes-Benz, Audi, Porsche, Volvo, Jaguar, MINI, Bentley, Rolls-Royce, Ferrari, Lamborghini, Maserati, Lotus, Tesla) are excluded by design.
• Blank cells represent unavailable/not-applicable data — never the text 'NULL'.
• All monetary values are in Indian Rupees (INR), stored as plain numerics (no currency symbols) for PostgreSQL compatibility.
• units_sold reflects VAHAN new-registration volumes, which is the industry-standard proxy for retail sales in India.

• Where an official monthly registration/wholesale figure is not separately published per model, a verified annual total is distributed across the applicable months using typical Indian passenger-vehicle seasonality (festive-season peak Oct-Dec, monsoon dip Jun-Aug) and flagged data_quality = 'Estimated from Official Annual'.
• Suggested primary key: EV_Models (brand_name, model_name). Suggested composite key: EV_Monthly_Sales (brand_name, model_name, sales_year, sales_month), with a foreign key back to EV_Models.
• Recommended PostgreSQL load: staging COPY into TEXT columns, then CAST/TRIM into the typed schema above; trim whitespace and standardise brand/model casing before constraint checks.
• Recommended Streamlit use: EV_Models for spec comparisons/filters; EV_Monthly_Sales for time-series and trend charts; EV_Statistics as a pre-aggregated fallback/reference for KPI tiles.
